title: eschscholzia lemmonii var. asprella
intro: Eschscholzia lemmonii var. asprella, commonly known as Lemmon's poppy, is a perennial plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and is believed to have a positive effect on the digestive system and respiratory system. Eschscholzia lemmonii var. asprella can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
